frozen_components: pre-trained word vectors (GloVe or Paragram-SL999 used as input)
vocabulary and lexical constraint sets (PPDB, WordNet)
architecture.summary: Counter-fitting is a lightweight post-processing method that refines pre-trained word vectors by injecting linguistic constraints (synonymy and antonymy relations). It operates via an objective function with three terms: Antonym Repel (pushes antonymous words apart using cosine distance with margin), Synonym Attract (pulls synonymous words closer), and Vector Space Preservation (maintains the original vector topology by constraining updates to a neighborhood). The method runs stochastic gradient descent for 20 epochs on the constraint-violation losses, completing in under two minutes on commodity hardware.
trained_components: constraint weighting hyperparameters (α, β, γ set equal in experiments)

biblio.title: Counter-fitting Word Vectors to Linguistic Constraints

claims.statement: Applying counter-fitting to Paragram-SL999 vectors achieves state-of-the-art performance on SimLex-999
Counter-fitting improves GloVe vectors from 0.41 to 0.58 on SimLex-999
Counter-fitted Paragram vectors improve dialogue state tracking on Restaurants dataset from 73.2 to 73.5 (ensemble of four RNN models with semantic dictionaries)
Counter-fitted GloVe vectors improve dialogue state tracking on Tourist Information dataset from 60.9 to 62.8
The Antonym Repel term uses margin δ_a set to orthogonality (π/2); the Synonym Attract term uses margin δ_s set to 0
Counter-fitting exhibits relative insensitivity to neighborhood radius ρ, with values between 0.2 and 0.4 showing little difference in quality; ρ = 0.3 used in experiments

Injecting WordNet antonyms substantially improves both GloVe and Paragram vectors, whereas Paragram vectors (already exposed to PPDB) only improve with WordNet antonym injection, not with additional PPDB constraints

Secondary (indirect) interactions through the three objective terms fix errors not appearing in the linguistic constraints: eight of seventeen highest-error word pairs are fixed by counter-fitting, five of which do not appear in constraint sets
